judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are strong and comparable on (a)-(c), but Report 2 additionally catches the missing global/environment-level gate (d), making it the more complete answer.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

report_2.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled()` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

report_1.evidence: I'd change the default at exactly `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12`, flipping the "missing means" branch to return `False` (opt-in) rather than `True`

report_2.evidence: I'd fix it at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting the missing-value branch to return `False`

report_1.evidence: `auto_send_reminders_enabled` was added by literally copying the shape of the pre-existing `compact_calendar_view_enabled` function from commit `25509a7`... the two flags have very different risk profiles (UI cosmetics vs. sending real messages to patients), and the default-on convention should not have been carried over without re-evaluating it

report_2.evidence: commit `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference") introduced the file with `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... Commit `aa4cf8d`... added `auto_send_reminders_enabled`... using the exact same `is None: return True` structure verbatim... a flag whose blast radius (unsolicited patient contact) is completely different from a calendar display preference

Confirm there's no env-level master switch gating the job globally (I found none)... a global kill switch AND-ed with the per-tenant value gives a fast, safe rollback path; right now the only way to stop sends is to edit every tenant record or disable the batch job entirely.
